message:
fix: QUIC closed-state guard, Sleep deadline drift, Notify spurious wakeup test, and minor cleanups

Multiple targeted fixes across the networking and sync subsystems:

- quic_native/connection.rs: Add explicit closed-state check in
  ensure_data_state() before the 1-RTT check. Previously, operations
  on a closed connection would report "1-RTT traffic not yet enabled"
  which was misleading — now correctly reports "connection is closed".
  Three existing tests updated to match the new error message.

- time/sleep.rs: Fix deadline drift in fallback thread sleep. The old
  approach computed remaining time from a wall-clock Instant::now()
  start point, which drifted if park_timeout returned early. Now uses
  the Sleep's actual deadline and time_getter to compute remaining
  time on each iteration, ensuring the fallback thread wakes at the
  correct absolute deadline. Also replace ready.store(false) with
  Arc::new(AtomicBool) on reset to avoid stale-reference races with
  in-flight fallback threads.

- sync/notify.rs: Add test_spurious_wakeup_bug regression test verifying
  that dropping a broadcast-woken waiter (fut1) does not spuriously
  wake a subsequent waiter (fut2).

- sync/notify_bug_test.rs: Upgrade from println to assert! so the
  spurious wakeup check fails loudly rather than silently printing.

- stream/debounce.rs: Use const initialization for thread_local Cell
  and method reference for Cell::get (clippy suggestions).

- websocket/handshake.rs: Add #[allow(clippy::option_if_let_else)]
  to parse_with_trailing to suppress false positive.

- websocket_acceptor_trailing_bytes.rs: Add #![allow(missing_docs)]
  crate-level attribute.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
